Long ago, before the sky had stars, the night was a heavy, suffocating blanket. The people of the earth feared the dark, for it hid the Great Serpent, a creature made of cold winds and shadows. 

One evening, a young girl named Elara decided she had had enough of the fear. She climbed the highest mountain, carrying only a glowing ember from her village’s sacred hearth, kept alive in a carved hollow stone. When she reached the peak, the Great Serpent rose to swallow her, its jaws wide and freezing.

Instead of running, Elara struck the stone against the mountain's peak, shattering it. The ember burst into a million tiny sparks. The wind caught the sparks, scattering them across the vast, empty canvas of the night. They stuck to the dark, burning brightly.

The sudden light blinded the Great Serpent, forcing it to retreat into the deep caves of the earth, where it remains to this day. Elara did not return, but she became the moon, a quiet guardian watching over the sparks she cast. And so, the stars were born, a promise that even the deepest darkness can be broken by a single ember.
myths.Agent_2:
Long before humans walked the valleys, the mountains were guarded by a solitary spirit named Aella. Aella possessed a voice of unmatched clarity, capable of coaxing the spring blooms from the frost and calming the fiercest winter blizzards. One cycle, a wandering river god named Oros carved a new path through her canyon. Oros was restless, made of rushing water and tumbling stones. Aella fell deeply in love with his wild song.

She sang to him every dawn, and for a season, Oros lingered, pooling in the basin of her granite peaks. But a river cannot stay still forever. Yearning for the vast, uncharted ocean, Oros eventually broke through the limestone barrier and rushed away, leaving Aella behind.

Heartbroken, Aella called out his name, but only the rushing wind answered. She refused to leave her peak, waiting patiently for his return. As centuries passed, her physical form faded into the gray rock, leaving nothing behind but her eternal voice. To this day, if you stand in a deep canyon and call out, Aella will answer. She repeats your words, forever hoping that one day, the voice she hears will be that of her lost river god.
